item.title: Magic
item.description: I was describing the mark-up of a CSS rule to someone learning CSS. I though it would be handy to have a reference for it, so I created the following reference image for him. Lets break it down into parts: * The whole thing is a ruleset * The curly braces and everything inside is a declaration block * The bit before the opening curly brace is a selector * A declaration has two parts, separated by a colon and ending in a semicolon * Each declaration has a property (before the semi colon) and a value (after the semi colon)

item.title: Anatomy of a CSS rule
item.description: I was describing the mark-up of a CSS rule to someone learning CSS. I though it would be handy to have a reference for it, so I created the following reference image for him. Lets break it down into parts: * The whole thing is a ruleset * The curly braces and everything inside is a declaration block * The bit before the opening curly brace is a selector * A declaration has two parts, separated by a colon and ending in a semicolon * Each declaration has a property (before the semi colon) and a value (after the semi colon)
